questions.question: What are the five stages of the stress escalation cycle described in the lesson?

options.text: Denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance
Overcommitment, self-care drops, errors and inefficiency, self-criticism, compensatory overwork
Awareness, resistance, exhaustion, collapse, recovery
Trigger, reaction, avoidance, guilt, repeat

questions.explanation: The stress escalation cycle follows five stages: overcommitment (saying yes to too much), self-care drops (exercise, sleep, meals get squeezed out), errors and inefficiency (fatigue leads to mistakes), self-criticism ('I should be able to handle this'), and compensatory overwork (working harder, which feeds back to stage 1).

questions.question: What is a 'stress signature'?

options.text: A medical diagnosis for chronic stress
The unique way stress shows up in your body, thoughts, emotions, and behavior
A written commitment to manage stress better
The specific event that causes your stress

questions.explanation: Your stress signature is the unique way stress shows up in your body (tension, headaches, fatigue), thoughts (catastrophizing, perfectionism, rumination), emotions (irritability, anxiety, numbness), and behavior (procrastination, withdrawal, substance use). Everyone experiences stress differently.

questions.question: Why is the metaphor of a cup used to explain stress accumulation?

options.text: Because stress only affects you once you reach a specific breaking point
Because each stressor adds water, and the cup overflows not because of the last drop but because of everything already in it
Because drinking water helps reduce stress
Because you should measure your stress precisely like liquid in a cup

questions.explanation: Stress is cumulative, not comparative. Like a cup, each stressor adds water. The cup overflows not because of the last drop, but because of everything already in it. No single stressor may seem overwhelming on its own, but the combination makes stress chronic.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the most powerful skill in stress management?

options.text: Being able to eliminate all sources of stress
Working harder and more efficiently
Catching stress early through recognizing your personal early warning signs
Avoiding all stressful situations entirely

questions.explanation: The most powerful skill in stress management is catching it early. Early warning signs are subtle — they are the first indicators that your stress cup is filling up. Learning to notice these signals before they escalate gives you the most options for responding.

questions.question: What does the lesson say about the relationship between self-awareness and self-blame?

options.text: Identifying your stress patterns should motivate you to try harder
Self-blame is necessary to create lasting change
Identifying patterns is about awareness, not blame — your stress responses are signals, not character flaws
You should avoid examining your patterns because it creates guilt

questions.explanation: Identifying your stress patterns is about awareness, not blame. When you notice procrastination or irritability, the goal is not to criticize yourself — it is to recognize these as signals that your stress cup is full. Your stress responses are adaptive signals, not character flaws.

title: Identifying Your Stress Triggers and Patterns - Quiz
